data.title: SatixFy signs $9M software development and license agreement with MDA Space
data.description:
• SatixFy Communications (NYSE:SATX) on Tuesday said it has signed a software development and license agreement with MDA Space.
  • The full contract price is $9M, including $6M for delivery of the software solution with a license to support an existing MDA Space customer and $3M for a further license to support future customers.
  • In addition, the contract allows MDA Space to retain an option to be granted access to a part of the code, related specifically to the data plane, for a further $4M.
  • As per the agreement, SatixFy will provide the modem and beamforming software for chipsets to be used in digital satellite broadband Low Earth Orbit Constellation (LEO) payloads. In addition, it will also provide SatixFy’s software licenses and a development kit to support MDA Space deliveries to customers.
  • SATX +4.8% premarket to $0.65.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Blackwell 3D to cancel 25M shares of common stock
data.description:
• Blackwell 3D Construction (OTCPK:BDCC) announced, on Tuesday, a significant restructuring of its common stock. The company’s board has authorized the cancellation of 25M shares of common stock held by CEO, Mohammedsaif Zaveri.
  • In exchange for this cancellation, Zaveri shall receive 500,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock.
  • This strategic move is aimed at reinforcing the company’s capital structure, the company said.
  • The cancellation of 25M shares of common stock shall reduce the overall outstanding common share pool, thereby reducing shareholder dilution.
  • This cancellation is expected to be completed within 10 days, at which point the company’s total issued and outstanding shares will be reduced from 60,997,373 to 35,997,373.
  • Blackwell 3D (OTCPK:BDCC) shares were up more than 6.8% in pre-market trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Flag Ship Acquisition signs merger agreement with Great Rich Technologies, GRT Merger Star
data.description:
• Blank check firm Flag Ship Acquisition (NASDAQ:FSHP) on Monday has signed a merger agreement with Great Rich Technologies and GRT Merger Star ((Merger Sub)).
  • At the closing of the merger, Flag Ship shareholders will receive American depositary shares of GRT and Flag Ship will be merged into the Merger Sub, a wholly-owned subsidiary of GRT.
  • FSHP +0.50% premarket to $10.10.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Coya Therapeutics looks to raise $10M in private placement
data.description:
• Coya Therapeutics (NASDAQ:COYA) said it has looking to raise $10M through a private placement.
  • The firm said it will issue 1.38M shares at $7.25 apiece.
  • Shares in the company were up 0.7% in premarket trade.

data.title: AppLovin started with Buy rating at Loop Capital
data.description:
• AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP) was in focus on Tuesday as Loop Capital started coverage on the mobile software company with a Buy rating and $181 price target.
  • Shares rose 0.8% in premarket trading.
  • "AppLovin has become indispensable infrastructure for the mobile gaming industry and is proving itself as a big data and AI play for investors," analyst Rob Sanderson wrote in a note to clients. "For a sizable market cap with impressive growth, the stock was relatively undiscovered and, in our view, largely misunderstood until breaking out recently. Given the rapid stock price appreciation we would welcome [the] opportunity to buy on a pullback, but we think the revaluation is warranted, it will be sustainable, and we still see meaningful growth runway for the business within its core area of focus."
  • In addition, Sanderson said AppLovin has several parallels with The Trade Desk (TTD), but AppLovin's software business is bigger and growing with a "meaningfully higher" margin. And the expansion into ecommerce ads could boost the company's total addressable market, Sanderson said.

data.title: Trimas declares $0.04 dividend
data.description:
• Trimas (NASDAQ:TRS) declared $0.04/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.57%
  • Payable Nov. 12; for shareholders of record Nov. 5; ex-div Nov. 5.
  • See TRS Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Trump Media launches Truth+ app for Android TV
data.description:
• Trump Media & Technology Group (NASDAQ:DJT) said Tuesday it has launched an app for the Truth+ streaming platform that Android TV users can download from the Google Play Store.
  • The company on Monday launched an app for iOS mobile devices and for Apple TV users to access the Truth+.
  • Press Release.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures fall as investors exercise caution amid earnings season
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Tuesday, while yields were slightly higher, as investors grew cautious amid the earnings season, and as the U.S. presidential election nears.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.7% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.19%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.06%.

Wall Street's major averages closed out the session mixed on Monday, as they wait for significant earnings reports to be released throughout the week.

"Yesterday, equities struggled, particularly as bond yields kept on rising," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Yesterday’s pontification of Federal Reserve speakers created a stir in bond markets. There were a range of views expressed, from “keep on cutting” to “more gradual cuts”. Markets, rendered skittish by Fed’s “data dependency” on undependable data, have raised yields, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"With two weeks to go today until the election, markets have started the week a bit more nervously than during the last six, where the S&P 500 has gone up each week for only the second time since the pandemic," Reid added.

On the earnings front, General Electric (GE), General Motors (GM), and Verizon Communications (VZ), among others, are expected to report before the bell on Tuesday.

On the economic calendar, the October Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index is expected today, and is anticipated to come in at -19.

data.title: PNC announces redemption of notes
data.description:
• PNC Financial Services (NYSE:PNC) announced on Tuesday, the redemption of, on Nov. 4, 2024, all outstanding Senior Floating Rate Bank Notes due Dec. 2, 2024 issued by PNC Bank, National Association in the amount of $200M.
  • The securities have an original scheduled maturity date of Dec. 2, 2024. The redemption price will be equal to 100% of the principal amount, plus any accrued and unpaid interest to the redemption date of Nov. 4, 2024.
  • Interest on the Senior Floating Rate Bank Notes will cease to accrue on the redemption date.
  • PNC will also redeem, on Oct. 28, 2024, all outstanding 5.671% Fixed Rate/Floating Rate Senior Notes due Oct. 28, 2025 issued by PNC in the amount of $1B.
  • The securities have an original scheduled maturity date of Oct. 28, 2025.
  • Payment of the redemption price for the Senior Floating Rate Bank Notes and the 5.671% Fixed Rate/Floating Rate Senior Notes will be made through the facilities of the Depository Trust Company.

data.title: CytoSorbents rises as FDA accepts application for device to reduce bleeding risk in bypass surgery
data.description:
• CytoSorbents (NASDAQ:CTSO) announced the U.S. FDA has accepted its de novo application for DrugSorb-ATR, a device to reduce risk of bleeding in patients undergoing coronary artery bypass graft surgery and initiated substantive review.
  • Shares of the stock is up 7% premarket.
  • The FDA De Novo pathway provides a regulatory process for novel medical devices that are found to be low to moderate risk and lack an appropriate predicate device.
  • The company anticipates an FDA decision in 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
